# pfsense_certificate_authority (Data Source)

Retrieves a single [certificate authority](https://docs.netgate.com/pfsense/en/latest/certificates/cas.html) (CA) by descriptive name from the pfSense trust store.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "pfsense_certificate_authority" "example" {
  descr = "Homelab CA Root CA"
}

output "ca_subject" {
  value = data.pfsense_certificate_authority.example.subject
}

output "ca_valid_to" {
  value = data.pfsense_certificate_authority.example.valid_to
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `descr` (String) Descriptive name for the certificate authority.

### Read-Only

- `refid` (String) Unique reference ID assigned by pfSense.
- `certificate` (String, Sensitive) PEM-encoded CA certificate.
- `private_key` (String, Sensitive) PEM-encoded private key for the CA (optional, only needed for signing).
- `trust` (Boolean) Whether the CA is added to the operating system trust store.
- `random_serial` (Boolean) Whether to use random serial numbers for issued certificates.
- `next_serial` (Number) Next serial number to use when issuing certificates (sequential mode).
- `subject` (String) Certificate subject (distinguished name).
- `issuer` (String) Certificate issuer (distinguished name).
- `serial` (String) Certificate serial number.
- `has_private_key` (Boolean) Whether the CA has a private key stored.
- `is_self_signed` (Boolean) Whether the CA certificate is self-signed.
- `valid_from` (String) Certificate validity start date.
- `valid_to` (String) Certificate validity end date.
- `in_use` (Boolean) Whether the CA is referenced by certificates, CRLs, or other configuration.
